body.title: Web thread context injector: scope to current session only
body.result: context_session field in queue message + _get_thread_id preference; 4 tests; comms://igor routing unchanged
body.intention: I intend that the web server's thread context injector only includes exchanges from the current session, preventing old channel history from contaminating Igor's NarrativeEngine context.
body.description:
2026-06-06 investigation (T-igor-web-response-quality) confirmed: when Akien sent a message about The Librarian at 12:41:09, the web server at 12:41:44 prepended old channel history ([Thread context — recent exchanges in this channel:] User: I was once at...) from unrelated prior exchanges (Igor reading blog posts). Igor's NarrativeEngine responded to the injected history, not the actual message. The fix: thread context injector must scope to exchanges that share the current session start time, not all recent channel exchanges.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint; likely the web server / uc_deliver handler that builds the [Thread context...] prefix
**Design rules:** diagnose before fixing; web→Igor uses HTTP poll not IMAP
**Scope boundary:** In scope: filter thread context to current-session exchanges only. Out of scope: NarrativeEngine changes, changing context format, web UI changes.
**Completion criteria:** After fix, messages sent in a new web session do not include channel history from prior sessions or unrelated channel exchanges. Manual test: start fresh web session, send message, verify [Thread context] (if present) only contains current-session exchanges.
